docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
This project aims to develop a real-time, AI-assisted classroom discussion tool designed for discussion-based university lectures in the Department of Pharmacology. The system combines live speech-to-text transcription ("hearing"), presentation content analysis ("seeing"), and AI-driven prompt generation to support interactive, anonymous student participation during lectures.

Instructors use the platform to create and manage lessons, generate discussion prompts from live lecture content, and collect anonymous student responses via QR code or PIN-based access. Students participate through a lightweight web interface without requiring authentication, ensuring privacy and ease of access. After each session, the system automatically aggregates prompts, responses, transcripts, and analytics for instructor review and export.

The primary quality goals of the system are convenience, accuracy, and security, ensuring minimal disruption during live lectures while maintaining reliable AI output and protecting user data.

docs.title: Project Glossary
docs.text:
• Lesson – A single lecture session created and managed by an instructor.
  • Discussion Prompt – A question generated by AI or manually created by an instructor to initiate student discussion.
  • Session – A live instance of a lesson during which students can join and participate.
  • STT (Speech-to-Text) – Real-time transcription of instructor audio during a lecture.
  • QR Code / PIN – Mechanisms used by students to join a live lesson.
  • Anonymous Response – A student submission that is not linked to any identifiable user data.
  • Prompt State – The current lifecycle state of a discussion (e.g., open or closed).
  • Context Builder – A system layer that controls what lecture content is sent to the AI to reduce hallucinations and cost.

docs.title: US 1.01 – Create Account (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to create an account on the platform, so that I can access the system and manage my teaching materials.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor without an account WHEN they click "Sign in with Google" and select their UAlberta email THEN they are redirected to UAlberta SSO login

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they complete UAlberta SSO authentication successfully THEN their account is automatically created and they are logged into the system

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they attempt to sign in with a non-UAlberta Google account THEN authentication is blocked with error message "Please use your UAlberta email"

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they sign in with UAlberta email for the first time THEN they are directed to the instructor dashboard

docs.title: US 1.02 – Login via UAlberta Authentication (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to login via UAlberta authentication, so that I can access my materials and have instructor privileges.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with existing account WHEN they click "Sign in with Google" and authenticate via UAlberta SSO THEN they successfully login and access the instructor dashboard

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they fail UAlberta SSO authentication THEN login fails and they see error message "Authentication failed"

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they close and reopen the application within session timeout THEN they remain logged in without re-authenticating

docs.title: US 1.03 – Logout Securely (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to logout securely, so that I can protect my account when finished.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a logged-in instructor WHEN they click "Logout" from the profile page THEN they are logged out and session is terminated

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they logout THEN they are redirected to the login page

  3. GIVEN a logged-out instructor WHEN they attempt to access protected pages via URL THEN they are redirected to login page

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they logout THEN all session data is cleared from the browser

docs.title: US 1.04 – View Only Own Past Lessons (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want only I to be able to view my past lessons and details, so that my lessons remain private.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they access their dashboard THEN they see only their own past lessons, not other instructors' lessons

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they attempt to access another instructor's lesson via direct URL THEN access is denied with 403 Forbidden error

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view lesson details THEN only data from their own lessons is displayed

docs.title: US 1.05 – Create New Lesson (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to create a new lesson, so that I can prepare content for a lecture.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they click "Create Lesson" and provide a title THEN a new lesson is created with inactive status

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create a lesson without providing a title THEN the system generates a default title with current date/time

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create a new lesson THEN it appears in their lesson list

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create a lesson without uploading files THEN the lesson is still created successfully

docs.title: US 1.06 – Start a Lesson (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to start a lesson, so that students can join and I can begin teaching.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with a created lesson WHEN they click "Start Lesson" THEN the lesson status changes to active

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they start a lesson THEN a unique PIN code is generated and displayed

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they start a lesson THEN a unique QR code is generated and displayed

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN the lesson is active THEN students can join using the PIN or QR code

docs.title: US 1.07 – Cancel a Lesson (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to cancel a lesson, so that I can abort lesson creation if needed.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor creating a lesson WHEN they click "Cancel" THEN the lesson is not saved to their lesson list

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they cancel lesson creation THEN they are returned to the appropriate page

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they cancel a lesson THEN any uploaded files for that lesson are discarded

docs.title: US 1.08 – Delete Past Lesson (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to delete a past lesson, so that I can remove outdated or incorrect lessons.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select a past lesson and click "Delete" THEN a confirmation dialog appears

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they confirm deletion THEN the lesson is permanently removed from the system

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they delete a lesson THEN it no longer appears in their lesson list

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they delete a lesson THEN all associated data (prompts, responses, transcripts, files) is permanently deleted

docs.title: US 1.09 – End/Leave a Lesson (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to end/leave a lesson, so that the lesson is closed.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN they click "End/Leave Lesson" THEN the lesson status changes to inactive

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end/leave a lesson THEN students cannot submit new responses

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end/leave a lesson THEN students are notified that the lesson has ended

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end/leave a lesson THEN all lesson data (prompts, responses, transcripts) is saved automatically

  5. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end/leave a lesson THEN files, prompts, questions, and analytics are ready for export

docs.title: US 1.10 – Automatic Lesson Save on End (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want the lesson to be saved automatically when I end it, so that transcripts, prompts, and responses are preserved.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end a lesson THEN all lesson data is automatically saved without manual action required

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end a lesson THEN all discussion prompts with timestamps are preserved

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end a lesson THEN all student responses with submission times are preserved

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end a lesson and STT was used THEN all transcripts are preserved

  5. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they access a saved lesson THEN they can view all preserved data

docs.title: US 1.11 – Resume Unfinished Lesson (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to resume a previously unfinished lesson, so that I can continue where I left off.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view their dashboard THEN they can see a list of unfinished lessons separate from finished lessons

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select an unfinished lesson and click "Resume" THEN the lesson opens with all previous data intact

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they resume a lesson THEN previously uploaded files are available

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they resume a lesson THEN previously created discussions are available

docs.title: US 1.12 – Reconnect After Connection Failure (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to reconnect if my connection fails, so that I can continue my lesson where I left off.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN connection is lost THEN they can click "Reconnect" to rejoin

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they reconnect to active lesson THEN the lesson state (current discussion, student count) is preserved

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they reconnect THEN student responses submitted during disconnection are visible

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they reconnect THEN they see a message confirming successful reconnection

docs.title: US 1.13 – Automatic Data Saving at Regular Intervals (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to have my lesson data automatically saved at regular intervals, so that progress is not lost if something goes wrong.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN they are teaching THEN lesson data is automatically saved at regular intervals

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN connection is lost THEN discussion prompts created before disconnection are preserved

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN connection is lost THEN student responses submitted before disconnection are preserved

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they reconnect after unexpected disconnection THEN auto-saved data is recoverable

docs.title: US 1.14 – View Past Lessons and Details (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to view past lessons and its details, so that I can review transcripts, prompts, and responses.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they access their dashboard THEN they see a list of all past lessons

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select a past lesson THEN they can view the lesson details page

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN viewing past lesson details THEN they see all discussion prompts that were published

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN viewing past lesson details THEN they see all student responses organized by prompt

  5. GIVEN an instructor WHEN viewing past lesson details and STT was used THEN they see all transcripts

docs.title: US 1.15 – Helpful Error Messages (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to see helpful error messages when something goes wrong, so that I can troubleshoot issues.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN file upload fails THEN they see a clear error message indicating the failure reason

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI prompt generation fails THEN they see error message "Prompt generation failed. Please try again or enter manually"

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN connection is lost THEN they see error message "Connection lost. Attempting to reconnect..."

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN an error occurs THEN the error message is displayed prominently and clearly

docs.title: US 1.49 – Add Course to Courselist (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to add a course which contains all lessons specific to that course to the courselist, so that I can organize my lessons by course.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they click "Create Course" THEN they can enter a course name and optional description

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create a course THEN it appears in their courses list

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create or edit a lesson THEN they can assign it to an existing course

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view a course THEN they see all lessons assigned to that course

docs.title: US 1.50 – Delete Course from Courselist (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to delete a course which contains all the lessons specific to that course to the courselist, so that I can remove courses I no longer need.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select a course and click "Delete" THEN a confirmation dialog appears warning about lesson deletion

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they confirm course deletion THEN the course and all its lessons are permanently removed

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they confirm course deletion THEN all files and metadata associated with lessons in that course are permanently deleted

docs.title: US 1.16 – Upload Files (PDF, PPTX) (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to upload files (PDF, PPTX), so that I can provide sources for discussion prompts.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they upload a PDF file within size limit THEN the file is accepted and appears in the uploaded files list

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they upload a PPTX file within size limit THEN the file is accepted and appears in the uploaded files list

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they attempt to upload an unsupported file type (e.g., .docx, .txt) THEN upload fails with error message "Unsupported file type. Please upload PDF or PPTX"

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they upload files THEN they can see a list of all uploaded files with names and types

  5. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they upload a file THEN the system accepts only 1 file per lesson

docs.title: US 1.17 – Activate/Deactivate STT Transcript Capture (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to activate and deactivate STT transcript capture, so that I can control when my spoken content is recorded.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN they click "Start Recording" THEN the recording period begins and STT transcript capture activates

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN recording period is active THEN their speech is transcribed in real-time

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they click "Stop Recording" THEN the recording period ends and STT transcript capture deactivates

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they stop recording THEN the transcript is sent to AI for prompt generation

  5. GIVEN an instructor WHEN recording period is active THEN they can toggle STT on and off multiple times during the lesson

docs.title: US 1.18 – Trigger AI Prompt Generation (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to trigger AI prompt generation, so that the system creates discussion questions based on my content.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with uploaded files WHEN they click "Generate Prompts" THEN AI processes the files and generates discussion prompts

  2. GIVEN an instructor with completed transcript WHEN recording period ends THEN AI automatically generates prompts from the transcript and files

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI is generating prompts THEN they see a loading indicator

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI completes generation THEN at least 3 discussion prompts are displayed

docs.title: US 1.19 – Review and Select AI Generated Prompts (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to review and select AI generated prompts, so that I can choose the most appropriate discussion question.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI generates prompts THEN all generated prompts are displayed for review

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view generated prompts THEN they can select one prompt from the options

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select a prompt THEN it is highlighted or visually marked

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select a prompt THEN they can change their selection before publishing

docs.title: US 1.20 – Edit AI-Generated Prompts (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to edit AI-generated prompts before publishing, so that I can refine the wording.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with selected AI prompt WHEN they click "Edit" THEN the prompt text becomes editable

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they modify the prompt text THEN the changes are saved

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they edit a prompt THEN they can see both original and edited versions

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they edit a prompt THEN they can revert to the original AI-generated text

docs.title: US 1.21 – Manually Enter Prompt (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want the option to manually enter a prompt, so that I can continue the discussion even if AI fails.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they click "Create Custom Prompt" THEN a text input field appears for manual entry

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they manually enter a prompt THEN it functions the same as AI-generated prompts

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create a manual prompt THEN they can publish it without triggering AI generation

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI generation fails THEN they see option to manually enter a prompt

docs.title: US 1.22 – Set AI Prompt Generation Preferences (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to set preferences for how AI generates prompts, so that prompts align with my teaching style.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they access AI settings THEN they can configure prompt generation preferences

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they set preferences THEN AI uses these preferences in future prompt generations

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they change preferences THEN the new settings are saved and applied to subsequent generations

docs.title: US 1.23 – Generate Multiple Choice, Short Answer, and Long Answer Prompts (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to generate multiple choice, short answer, and long answer type discussion prompts, so that I can vary discussion formats.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they trigger AI generation THEN they can specify prompt type (multiple choice, short answer, or long answer)

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select multiple choice format THEN AI generates a question with answer options

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select short answer or long answer format THEN AI generates an open-ended question

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they manually create a prompt THEN they can choose between multiple choice, short answer, or long answer formats

docs.title: US 1.24 – Regenerate AI Prompts (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to regenerate AI prompts if I'm not satisfied, so that I can get alternative suggestions.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with AI-generated prompts WHEN they click "Regenerate" THEN AI generates a new set of prompts

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they regenerate prompts THEN the previous prompts are replaced with new ones

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they regenerate THEN they see a loading indicator while AI generates new prompts

docs.title: US 1.25 – Create Multiple Discussions Within One Lesson (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to create multiple discussions within one lesson, so that I can facilitate ongoing engagement throughout my lecture.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN they publish a discussion prompt THEN they can create additional discussions in the same lesson

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they create multiple discussions THEN each discussion is tracked separately

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view lesson data THEN they see all discussions that occurred during the lesson

docs.title: US 1.26 – Private Discussion Prompts per Lesson (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want only students in my lesson to be able to see my discussion prompts, so that discussions remain private to my lesson.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN they publish a discussion prompt THEN only students who joined via that lesson's PIN/QR code can see the prompt

  2. GIVEN students in different lessons WHEN an instructor publishes a prompt THEN students only see prompts from their own lesson

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they publish a prompt THEN it is not visible to students in other instructors' lessons

docs.title: US 1.27 – Display Discussion Prompt (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to display the discussion prompt, so that students can see the question.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with selected/created prompt WHEN they click "Publish" or "Display" THEN the prompt is sent to all connected students

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they display a prompt THEN students see it on their devices within 2 seconds

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they display a prompt THEN it appears in the lesson's discussion history

docs.title: US 1.28 – Start and Close Discussions (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to start and close discussions within a lesson, so that I can control when students can respond.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they publish a discussion prompt THEN the response window opens and students can submit responses

  2. GIVEN an instructor with open discussion WHEN they click "Close Discussion" THEN the response window closes and students can no longer submit

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they close a discussion THEN students see a message indicating the discussion is closed

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they close a discussion THEN they can start a new discussion

docs.title: US 1.29 – Set Time Limit for Response Window (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to set a time limit for a response window, so that I can control how long students have to submit their answers.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor creating a discussion WHEN they enable time limit THEN they can specify duration in minutes and seconds

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they set a time limit THEN students see a countdown timer on their devices

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN the time limit expires THEN the response window automatically closes and students cannot submit

  4. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they publish discussion without time limit THEN students can respond until instructor manually closes the discussion

docs.title: US 1.30 – Configure Single or Multiple Responses (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to configure whether students can submit one or multiple responses per discussion, so that I can control participation based on the type of question.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor creating a discussion WHEN they configure response settings THEN they can choose "Single Response" or "Multiple Responses"

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select "Single Response" THEN each student can only submit one response to that discussion

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they select "Multiple Responses" THEN students can submit multiple responses to that discussion

docs.title: US 1.31 – Display PIN Code (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to display a PIN code for students to join the lesson, so that students can access my session.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they start a lesson THEN a unique PIN code is generated

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN the lesson is active THEN the PIN code is displayed prominently on their screen

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end the lesson THEN the PIN code is invalidated and cannot be used to join

docs.title: US 1.32 – Display QR Code (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to display a QR code for students to join the lesson, so that students can quickly join by scanning.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they start a lesson THEN a unique QR code is generated

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN the lesson is active THEN the QR code is displayed on their screen for students to scan

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they end the lesson THEN the QR code is invalidated and cannot be used to join

docs.title: US 1.33 – See Student Count (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to see how many students have joined, so that I can gauge attendance.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with active lesson WHEN students join THEN the student count updates in real-time

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view their lesson dashboard THEN they see the number of currently connected students

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN a student leaves THEN the student count decrements

docs.title: US 1.34 – See Anonymous Student Responses in Real-Time (Must Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to see anonymous student responses in real-time, so that I can monitor engagement and understanding.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with open discussion WHEN students submit responses THEN the responses appear in real-time without manual refresh

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view responses THEN each response is displayed anonymously without student identifiers

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN multiple students submit THEN all responses appear in the list as they are submitted

docs.title: US 1.35 – Hide Inappropriate Responses (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to hide inappropriate responses, so that I can moderate discussion content.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor viewing responses WHEN they select a response and click "Hide" THEN that response is removed from the visible list

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they hide a response THEN it is no longer visible to them or to students (if responses are shared)

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they hide a response THEN the action is logged but the original response data is preserved

docs.title: US 1.36 – Highlight Specific Response (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to highlight a specific response, so that I can emphasize answers during class discussion.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor viewing responses WHEN they select a response and click "Highlight" THEN that response is visually emphasized

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they highlight a response THEN it remains highlighted until they remove the highlight or close the discussion

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they highlight a response THEN they can highlight additional responses or remove existing highlights

docs.title: US 1.37 – Scroll Through Student Responses (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to scroll through student responses, so that I can review all submissions.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN students submit responses THEN all responses are displayed in a scrollable list

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN there are many responses THEN they can scroll to view all submissions

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they scroll through responses THEN each response remains readable and properly formatted

docs.title: US 1.38 – View Responses as Word Cloud (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to choose to view responses as a word cloud format, so that I can visualize common themes at a glance.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor viewing responses WHEN they select "Word Cloud" view THEN responses are displayed as a word cloud showing common terms

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view word cloud THEN more frequently used words appear larger

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they switch to word cloud view THEN they can switch back to list view

docs.title: US 1.39 – View Responses in List Format (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to choose to view responses in a list format, so that I can read them sequentially.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor viewing responses WHEN they select "List" view THEN all responses are displayed in a sequential list

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view list format THEN each response is displayed with timestamp

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they are in list view THEN they can switch to other view formats (e.g., word cloud)

docs.title: US 1.40 – See Student Response Metrics (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to see metrics of student responses, so that I can view engagement rate and participation statistics.

Points assigned: 5

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with closed discussion WHEN they view analytics THEN they see participation rate (responses submitted / students connected)

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view metrics THEN they see average response time and submission patterns

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view metrics THEN they see response length distribution

docs.title: US 1.41 – Export Responses as File (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to export responses as a file, so that I can access and analyze them outside of the platform.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with lesson data WHEN they click "Export Responses" THEN they can choose export format (CSV, PDF, or JSON)

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export as CSV THEN the file contains all responses with timestamps in structured format

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export THEN the file downloads immediately to their device

docs.title: US 1.42 – Export AI Prompts and Responses (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to export AI generated prompts and its responses as a file, so that I can access and analyze them outside of the platform.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with lesson data WHEN they click "Export Prompts and Responses" THEN they can choose export format

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export THEN the file contains all prompts with their associated responses

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export THEN the file includes timestamps for prompts and responses

docs.title: US 1.43 – Export Lesson Statistics (Should Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to export appropriate lesson statistics, so that I can access and analyze them outside of the platform.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with completed lesson WHEN they click "Export Statistics" THEN they can choose what statistics to include

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export statistics THEN the file includes participation metrics, response counts, and timing data

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they export statistics THEN the file downloads in their chosen format

docs.title: US 1.44 – See Multiple Choice Question Metrics (Could Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to see multiple choice question metrics, so that I can view statistics like answer distribution and correctness rates.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor with multiple choice discussion WHEN they view results THEN they see how many students selected each option

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view MC metrics THEN they see percentage distribution of answers

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they view MC metrics THEN they see which option was marked as correct and the overall correctness rate

docs.title: US 1.45 – Customized UI Graphics (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to see customized graphics in the UI for instructors, so that the interface is visually appealing.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they login THEN they see instructor-specific UI graphics and branding

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they use desktop THEN graphics display correctly at desktop resolutions

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they use mobile devices THEN graphics are responsive and display correctly

docs.title: US 1.46 – Fine-Tune AI with Trigger Words (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to fine-tune the AI prompt using trigger words, so that I can control prompt generation more precisely.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor on AI settings WHEN they add trigger words THEN those words are saved to the lesson or profile settings

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they say the trigger word THEN the AI prompt generation occurs

  3. GIVEN an instructor WHEN no trigger words are configured THEN default AI prompt generation behavior is used

docs.title: US 1.47 – Save and Export Student Chat Responses (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to save and export student chat responses, so that I can review them later.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor WHEN they click "Export Chat Responses" THEN the system exports chat data in the selected format (CSV, JSON, or PDF)

  2. GIVEN an active lesson with chat enabled WHEN students send chat messages THEN messages are stored with timestamps

docs.title: US 1.48 – Upload Multiple Files (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As an instructor, I want to be able to upload multiple files to support discussion prompt generation, so that I can provide comprehensive source material.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN an instructor creating or editing a lesson WHEN they upload multiple supported files (PDF/PPTX) THEN all files are accepted and listed

  2. GIVEN an instructor WHEN AI prompt generation is triggered THEN the system uses content from all uploaded files

docs.title: US 2.01 – Access Website on Desktop (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to access the website on desktop, so that I can participate from my computer or laptop.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they access the website URL on desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) THEN the page loads successfully

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they view the website on desktop THEN all interface elements display correctly

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they use desktop THEN all features (joining, responding, viewing prompts) work correctly

docs.title: US 2.02 – Access Website on Mobile Device (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to access the website on mobile device, so that I can participate from my phone or tablet.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they access the website URL on mobile browsers THEN the page loads successfully

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they view the website on mobile THEN the interface is responsive and fits the screen

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they use mobile THEN all features (joining, responding, viewing prompts) work with touch input

docs.title: US 2.03 – Complete Anonymity (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be completely anonymous, so that I can share honest responses without fear of judgment.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they join a lesson THEN they are not required to provide name, email, or any identifying information

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit responses THEN the responses contain no identifiable data

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor views responses THEN the instructor cannot trace responses back to individual students

  4. GIVEN a student WHEN lesson data is exported THEN the export contains no student identifiers

docs.title: US 2.04 – See Only Own Lesson Prompts (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to only see the prompts for the lesson I am in, so that I'm not confused by other lessons.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they join a lesson using PIN/QR code THEN they see only prompts for that specific lesson

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor publishes a prompt THEN they see only that lesson's prompt, not prompts from other active lessons

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they rejoin the same lesson THEN they see the current active prompt for that lesson

docs.title: US 2.05 – Scan QR Code to Join (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to scan a QR code and join the lesson, so that I can quickly join using my device's native camera.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they scan QR code with mobile device camera THEN they are directed to the lesson join page

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN QR code is valid and lesson is active THEN they successfully join the lesson

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they scan invalid or expired QR code THEN they see error message "Invalid or expired QR code"

docs.title: US 2.06 – Input PIN Code to Join (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to input a PIN code and join the lesson, so that I can access the lesson without scanning.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they enter correct PIN code THEN they successfully join the lesson

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they enter incorrect PIN THEN they see error message "Invalid PIN. Please try again"

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they enter expired PIN (lesson ended) THEN they see error message "This lesson has ended"

docs.title: US 2.07 – Submit Responses to Discussion Prompts (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to submit responses to discussion prompts, so that I can participate in class discussions.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student with open discussion WHEN they type a response and click "Submit" THEN the response is sent to the instructor

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit a response THEN they see confirmation message "Response submitted"

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they attempt to submit empty response THEN submission is blocked with message "Please enter a response"

docs.title: US 2.08 – Select Option for Multiple Choice Questions (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to select an option for multiple choice questions, so that I can answer quiz-style prompts.

Points assigned: 2

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor publishes multiple choice question THEN they see all answer options

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they select an option THEN it is visually highlighted

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they click "Submit" with selection THEN their answer is sent to the instructor

  4. GIVEN a student WHEN they attempt to submit without selecting THEN submission is blocked with message "Please select an answer"

docs.title: US 2.09 – See Discussion Prompt on Device (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to see the discussion prompt on my device, so that I know what to respond to.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor publishes a prompt THEN the prompt appears on their device within 2 seconds

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they view the prompt THEN it is clearly visible and readable

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor publishes new prompt THEN the previous prompt is replaced with the new one

docs.title: US 2.10 – See Multiple Choice Correctness (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to see if I got multiple choice questions correct, so that I can receive immediate feedback on my understanding.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit multiple choice answer and feedback is enabled THEN they see whether their answer was correct or incorrect

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN answer is correct THEN they see confirmation message indicating correctness

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN answer is incorrect THEN they see the correct answer displayed

docs.title: US 2.11 – See Time Remaining for Prompt (Should Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to see how much time a prompt has left to be answered, so that I can pace my response appropriately.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor sets time limit for discussion THEN they see a countdown timer

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN timer is active THEN it updates in real-time

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN time expires THEN they see message indicating time is up and cannot submit response

docs.title: US 2.18 – Submit Chat Responses to Others (Won't Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to submit chat responses to other students and the instructor during discussions, so that I can engage in peer-to-peer discussion.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student in an active lesson WHEN they send a chat message THEN the message appears in the shared lesson chat feed

  2. GIVEN participants in the same lesson WHEN a student posts a message THEN other participants receive it in real time

docs.title: US 2.12 – Notification When Lesson Ends (Should Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be notified when the lesson has ended, so that I know the lesson is complete and I can close the application.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN instructor ends the lesson THEN they see notification "Lesson has ended"

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN lesson ends THEN they cannot submit new responses

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN lesson ends THEN they see an appropriate end-of-lesson message and screen

docs.title: US 2.13 – Leave a Lesson (Should Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to leave a lesson, so that I can exit if needed.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student in active lesson WHEN they click "Leave Lesson" THEN they are disconnected from the lesson

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they leave THEN they are removed from the student count

docs.title: US 2.14 – Rejoin a Lesson (Must Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to rejoin a lesson, so that I can reconnect if I was disconnected or left temporarily.

Points assigned: 3

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they rejoin using same PIN/QR code and lesson is still active THEN they reconnect to the lesson

  2. GIVEN a student disconnected during a discussion WHEN they rejoin THEN they see the current active prompt

  3. GIVEN a student disconnected during a discussion WHEN they rejoin THEN they can submit responses to the current discussion

  4. GIVEN a student WHEN they attempt to rejoin ended lesson THEN they see error message "This lesson has ended"

docs.title: US 2.15 – See Lesson and Discussion Status (Should Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to see the status of the lesson and discussion, so that I know what actions I can take.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN discussion is open for responses THEN they see indicator showing discussion is open

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN discussion is closed THEN they see indicator showing discussion is closed

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they view status THEN it updates in real-time as instructor changes discussion state

docs.title: US 2.16 – Helpful Error Messages for Students (Could Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to see helpful error messages when something goes wrong, so that I know how to resolve issues.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they enter invalid PIN THEN they see clear error message with guidance

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they experience connection issue THEN they see error message "Connection lost. Attempting to reconnect..."

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN response submission fails THEN they see error message "Submission failed. Please try again"

docs.title: US 2.17 – Confirmation Message on Response Submission (Could Have)
docs.text:
As a student, I want to be able to see a confirmation message that I submitted my response, so that I know my answer was received.

Points assigned: 1

Acceptance Tests:

  1. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit a response THEN they see confirmation message immediately

  2. GIVEN a student WHEN they see confirmation THEN the message is in a textbox showing the confirmation

  3. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit THEN the submit button is disabled to prevent duplicate submissions

  4. GIVEN a student WHEN they submit THEN the confirmation message disappears after a brief moment

docs.title: Kahoot
docs.text:
• Core functionality: Live classroom quizzes, fast join via PIN, real-time response aggregation, instant result display.
  • Why relevant to our project: Matches our live instructor-led flow and low-friction student entry.
  • What we will imitate:
  • PIN-based join flow and clear “session state” UX (waiting/open/closed).
  • Real-time response updates for instructor dashboard.
  • What we will not imitate:
  • Game-heavy scoring/leaderboards (not aligned with discussion-focused teaching).

docs.title: Jackbox Games
docs.text:
• Core functionality: Host-controlled room sessions, player join by room code, synchronized multi-user state.
  • Why relevant to our project: Strong model for host/student role separation and robust session control.
  • What we will imitate:
  • Instructor as authoritative host for opening/closing prompts.
  • Reliable code-based joining and reconnect behavior.
  • What we will not imitate:
  • Entertainment-focused UI mechanics not needed for academic use.

docs.title: NotebookLM
docs.text:
• Core functionality: Upload source materials, AI-grounded synthesis from those sources, structured outputs.
  • Why relevant to our project: Similar to our prompt generation from lecture files/transcripts.
  • What we will imitate:
  • Source-grounded AI prompting pipeline to reduce hallucinations.
  • Content-ingestion step before generation (file/transcript preprocessing).
  • What we will not imitate:
  • General-purpose note workspace features outside lesson flow.

docs.title: Cluely
docs.text:
• Core functionality: In-class participation prompts, engagement tracking, response collection.
  • Why relevant to our project: Similar educational context and participation goals.
  • What we will imitate:
  • Lightweight student response UI and engagement indicators.
  • Instructor-side visibility of participation trends.
  • What we will not imitate:
  • Features that require student identities (our design prioritizes anonymity).

docs.title: Open-source Projects
docs.text: • Open Notebook: We’re using Open Notebook as a technical reference since it’s a proven open-source system that already handles the complex challenge of processing multiple document types (PDFs, presentations, papers) and using AI to generate responses from that content, similar to Google’s NotebookLM. It has an MIT license.

docs.title: API & Supabase Guide
docs.text: This project uses a small set of Next.js App Router API endpoints and relies on Supabase for auth, database access, and realtime events. Below is an OpenAPI 3.0 overview plus implementation notes.

docs.title: OpenAPI specification
docs.text:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: PMCOL Teaching Tool API
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Minimal server endpoints; most data access happens directly from the browser via Supabase.
servers:
  - url: https://ualberta-cmput401.github.io/W26project-DeptofPharmacology
    description: Deployed site (static; API hosted with the app runtime)
  - url: http://localhost:3000
    description: Local dev server
paths:
  /api/auth/callback:
    get:
      summary: Exchange Supabase OAuth code for a session
      parameters:
        - name: code
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Authorization code from Supabase OAuth provider
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirects to instructor dashboard on success
          headers:
            Location:
              schema:
                type: string
              description: Redirect target (e.g., /instructor_dashboard)
        '302_error':
          description: Redirects to /create_instructor?error=<message> when Supabase returns an error
      security: []
  /api/socket:
    get:
      summary: Placeholder endpoint for Socket.io
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Indicates placeholder or connected state
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
      security: []

docs.title: Supabase configuration
docs.text:
• Public keys live in app/.env.local (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY).
  • Browser client factory: app/src/lib/supabase/client.ts.
  • Server client with cookie-based auth: app/src/lib/supabase/server.ts.
  • Auth helpers (email/password, Google OAuth redirect to /api/auth/callback): app/src/lib/supabase/auth.ts.

docs.title: Data access (client → Supabase)
docs.text:
These functions call Supabase directly from the frontend using the anon key: - Courses: app/src/services/courseService.ts - Lessons: app/src/services/lessonService.ts - Discussions: app/src/services/discussionService.ts - Responses: app/src/services/responseService.ts

Refer to the TypeScript models in app/src/types/*.ts for field names used in the Supabase tables (instructors, courses, lessons, discussions, responses). Additional type files include instructor.ts and user.ts.

docs.title: Realtime
docs.text:
• Channel naming: lesson:{lessonId}
  • Managed by hook app/src/lib/realtime/useRealtime.ts with broadcast ack enabled.
  • Used for live updates between instructors and students within a lesson.

docs.title: Swagger UI preview
docs.text:
• Install deps (once): cd app && npm install
  • Serve the OpenAPI spec with Swagger UI: bash cd app npm run api:swagger Opens http://127.0.0.1:8080 using docs/openapi.yaml with live reload.

docs.title: Change log notes
docs.text:
• /api/auth/callback uses the server Supabase client and awaits exchangeCodeForSession.
  • OAuth redirects now go through /api/auth/callback to ensure cookies are set server-side.

docs.title: CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text: This document describes the Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipeline for the PMCOL Teaching Tool project.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The project uses GitHub Actions for automated testing, linting, type checking, and building. The CI pipeline runs on every push and pull request to the sprint-2 and main branches, ensuring code quality and preventing regressions before merging.

The pipeline is defined in .github/workflows/ci.yml and consists of multiple parallel jobs that validate different aspects of the codebase.

docs.title: 1. Setup
docs.text:
• Purpose: Install project dependencies
  • Working Directory: app/
  • Steps:
  • Checkout code
  • Setup Node.js 20 with npm cache
  • Run npm ci for clean dependency installation

docs.title: 2. Lint
docs.text:
• Purpose: Enforce code style and catch common errors
  • Dependencies: Requires setup job
  • Steps:
  • Run ESLint: npm run lint
  • What it checks:
  • Code formatting consistency
  • TypeScript/JavaScript best practices
  • Potential bugs and anti-patterns

docs.title: 3. Typecheck
docs.text:
• Purpose: Validate TypeScript types without emitting files
  • Dependencies: Requires setup job
  • Steps:
  • Run TypeScript compiler: npx tsc --noEmit
  • What it checks:
  • Type safety across the codebase
  • Interface compatibility
  • Proper type annotations

docs.title: 4. Jest Tests
docs.text:
• Purpose: Run unit and integration tests
  • Dependencies: Requires setup job
  • Steps:
  • Run Jest: npm test
  • Upload coverage report (if generated)
  • What it tests:
  • Component logic
  • Service functions
  • API utilities
  • Data transformations

docs.title: 5. Playwright UI Tests
docs.text:
• Purpose: Run end-to-end browser tests
  • Dependencies: Requires setup job
  • Environment Variables:
  • NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (from secrets)
  • NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (from secrets)
  • Steps:
  • Cache Playwright browsers
  • Build the Next.js app: npm run build
  • Free port 3000 (self-hosted cleanup)
  • Start Next.js server in background on port 3000
  • Wait for server to be ready (30 attempts, 2s intervals)
  • Run Playwright tests: npm run test:ui
  • Stop Next.js server
  • Upload test artifacts and logs
  • What it tests:
  • User flows (instructor/student journeys)
  • UI interactions
  • Real-time features
  • Authentication flows

docs.title: 6. Build
docs.text:
• Purpose: Verify production build succeeds
  • Dependencies: Requires lint, typecheck, test-jest, test-ui
  • Steps:
  • Run production build: npm run build
  • What it validates:
  • All code compiles successfully
  • No build-time errors
  • Production bundle is viable

docs.title: Trigger Conditions
docs.text:
The CI pipeline runs when:

  1. Push to sprint-2 or main branches
  2. Pull Request opened/updated against sprint-2 or main
  3. Manual trigger via workflow_dispatch

docs.title: Skip CI
docs.text:
To skip CI for a commit (e.g., documentation-only changes), include [skip ci] in the commit message:

git commit -m "Update README [skip ci]"

docs.title: Concurrency Control
docs.text:
The pipeline uses concurrency groups to cancel in-progress runs when new commits are pushed:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

This prevents resource waste and ensures only the latest code is tested.

docs.title: Runner Configuration
docs.text:
• Runner Type: self-hosted
  • Working Directory: app/ (for all jobs)
  • Node Version: 20.x
  • Cache: npm dependencies are cached to speed up runs

docs.title: Artifacts
docs.text:
The pipeline generates and uploads the following artifacts:

Artifact Name Contents When Uploaded jest-coverage Code coverage reports After Jest tests (if coverage exists) playwright-artifacts HTML reports and test results After Playwright tests (always) next-start-log Next.js server startup logs After Playwright tests (always)

Artifacts are available for 90 days by default and can be downloaded from the GitHub Actions run page.

docs.title: Required Secrets
docs.text:
The following secrets must be configured in GitHub repository settings:

  • NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL – Supabase project URL
  • NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY – Supabase anonymous key

docs.title: Setting Up Secrets
docs.text:
1. Go to Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions
  2. Click New repository secret
  3. Add each required secret

docs.title: Local Testing
docs.text:
Before pushing code, you can run the same checks locally:

cd app

# Install dependencies
npm ci

# Run linter
npm run lint

# Run type checker
npx tsc --noEmit

# Run Jest tests
npm test

# Run Playwright tests
npm run build
npm run start &  # Start server in background
npm run test:ui

docs.title: Port 3000 Already in Use
docs.text:
If the Playwright job fails with port conflicts on self-hosted runners:

# Kill process on port 3000
lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9
# or
fuser -k 3000/tcp

The CI pipeline includes automatic cleanup, but manual intervention may be needed on self-hosted runners.

docs.title: Playwright Browser Installation
docs.text:
If browsers are not cached:

cd app
npx playwright install chromium --no-shell

docs.title: Failed Build Step
docs.text: Check the build logs for: - Missing environment variables - TypeScript errors - Import/export issues

docs.title: Branch Protection Rules
docs.text:
To enforce CI before merging:

  1. Go to Settings → Branches → Branch protection rules
  2. Add rule for main and sprint-* branches
  3. Enable:
  4. ✅ Require status checks to pass before merging
  5. ✅ Require branches to be up to date before merging
  6. Select required checks: Lint, TypeScript typecheck, Jest tests, Playwright UI tests, Build

docs.title: Future Enhancements
docs.text:
Potential improvements to the CI/CD pipeline:

  • Deployment: Add CD stage to deploy to staging/production
  • Performance Testing: Add Lighthouse CI for performance regression detection
  • Security Scanning: Integrate Snyk or Dependabot for vulnerability scanning
  • Docker: Containerize the application for consistent environments
  • Preview Deployments: Auto-deploy PR previews to Vercel/Netlify

docs.text:

  • Jest Configuration

docs.title: Software Design
docs.text: This document describes the high-level software design of the PMCOL Teaching Tool, focusing on the system architecture, core domain entities, and key interaction scenarios. The system follows a layered architecture with a React-based web frontend, a Next.js backend providing API routes and real-time WebSocket communication, and external services for authentication, speech-to-text transcription, and AI-driven prompt generation. Persistent data storage and real-time synchronization are handled through Supabase. The included diagrams illustrate how instructors and students interact with the system, how lessons and discussions are managed securely and anonymously, and how data flows through the system to support real-time classroom engagement.

Low-Fidelity User Interface

The low-fidelity user interface designs were created in Figma to explore and validate the core user flows for both instructors and students before implementation. These wireframes focus on layout, navigation, and system states rather than final visual styling.

docs.title: Interface Interactions and Module Communication
docs.text: This section provides detailed documentation of how all interfaces (APIs, modules, components, hooks, and services) interact with each other to deliver the complete functionality of the PMCOL Teaching Tool. Every interface is mapped to specific Sprint 2 user stories to ensure all planned functionalities are fully represented.

docs.title: System Architecture Overview
docs.text:
The application follows a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        PRESENTATION LAYER                        │
│  React Components (Pages, UI Components, Forms)                 │
│  - Renders UI                                                    │
│  - Handles user interactions                                     │
│  - Delegates business logic to hooks                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             ↕
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        STATE MANAGEMENT LAYER                    │
│  Custom Hooks (useSessionPage, useInstructorDashboard, etc.)    │
│  - Manages component state                                       │
│  - Orchestrates service calls                                    │
│  - Handles side effects (real-time, routing)                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             ↕
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        SERVICE LAYER                             │
│  Service Modules (courseService, lessonService, etc.)           │
│  - Encapsulates business logic                                   │
│  - Makes database calls via Supabase client                      │
│  - Returns typed responses                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             ↕
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        DATA ACCESS LAYER                         │
│  Supabase Clients (client.ts, server.ts, auth.ts)              │
│  - Communicates with Supabase backend                            │
│  - Handles authentication                                        │
│  - Manages database connections                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             ↕
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     REAL-TIME COMMUNICATION LAYER                │
│  Real-time Hooks (useRealtime, useSocket)                       │
│  - Manages WebSocket connections                                 │
│  - Broadcasts events                                             │
│  - Listens for real-time updates                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

// 1. SignUpForm Component (Presentation)
export function SignUpForm() {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<SignUpFormData>({...});

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();

    // Validation (US 1.01 - AT1)
    if (!formData.agreeToTerms) {
      setError('You must agree to the Terms and Conditions');
      return;
    }

    // Call auth service (US 1.01 - AT2)
    const { error } = await signUpWithEmail(
      formData.email,
      formData.password,
      formData.fullName
    );

    if (error) {
      setError(error.message);
      return;
    }

    // Show confirmation (US 1.01 - AT3)
    setConfirmedEmail(formData.email);
  };
}

// 2. Auth Service (Business Logic)
export async function signUpWithEmail(
  email: string,
  password: string,
  fullName: string
) {
  const supabase = createClient();

  // US 1.01 - AT4: Create account with metadata
  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
    email,
    password,
    options: {
      data: { full_name: fullName }
    },
  });

  return { data, error };
}

// 3. Supabase Client (Data Access)
export function createClient() {
  return createBrowserClient(
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
  );
}

// 1. InstructorDashboard Component (Presentation)
export function InstructorDashboard() {
  const {
    courses,
    loading,
    handleCreateCourse,
    openDeleteDialog,
    // ... other state
  } = useInstructorDashboard();

  return (
    <div>
      <InstructorDashboardHeader onAddCourse={openCreateDialog} />
      <CoursesSection 
        courses={courses}
        onDeleteCourse={openDeleteDialog}
      />
      {/* Dialogs */}
    </div>
  );
}

// 2. useInstructorDashboard Hook (State Management)
export function useInstructorDashboard() {
  const [courses, setCourses] = useState<Course[]>([]);
  const [courseToDelete, setCourseToDelete] = useState<string | null>(null);

  // Load courses on mount (US 1.04 - AT1)
  useEffect(() => {
    async function fetchCourses() {
      const user = await supabase.auth.getUser();
      const { data } = await courseService.listInstructorCourses(user.data.user!.id);
      setCourses(data || []);
    }
    fetchCourses();
  }, []);

  // US 1.49 - Create course
  const handleCreateCourse = async (input: CreateCourseInput) => {
    const user = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    const { data, error } = await courseService.createCourse(
      user.data.user!.id,
      input
    );

    if (!error && data) {
      setCourses(prev => [...prev, ...data]); // Update state
      closeCreateDialog();
    }
  };

  // US 1.50 - Delete course
  const confirmDelete = async () => {
    if (!courseToDelete) return;

    const { courseResult } = await courseService.deleteCourseCascade(
      courseToDelete
    );

    if (!courseResult.error) {
      setCourses(prev => prev.filter(c => c.id !== courseToDelete));
      closeDeleteDialog();
    }
  };

  return {
    courses,
    handleCreateCourse,
    openDeleteDialog: (id: string) => setCourseToDelete(id),
    confirmDelete,
    // ...
  };
}

// 3. courseService (Data Layer)
export async function createCourse(
  instructorId: string,
  input: CreateCourseInput
) {
  const supabase = createClient();

  // US 1.49 - AT2: Insert and return course
  return supabase
    .from('courses')
    .insert([{
      instructor_id: instructorId,
      title: input.title.trim(),
      image_url: input.image_url?.trim() || null,
    }])
    .select();
}

export async function deleteCourseCascade(courseId: string) {
  const supabase = createClient();

  // US 1.50 - AT2, AT3: Cascade delete lessons, then course
  const lessonsResult = await supabase
    .from('lessons')
    .delete()
    .eq('course_id', courseId);

  const courseResult = await supabase
    .from('courses')
    .delete()
    .eq('id', courseId);

  return { lessonsResult, courseResult };
}

// 1. SessionPage Component (Main Container)
export function SessionPage({ lessonId }: { lessonId: string }) {
  const vm = useSessionPage(lessonId);

  if (vm.loading) return <SessionLoading />;
  if (vm.notFound) return <SessionNotFound />;

  // Route to appropriate view based on lesson status
  if (vm.lesson.status === 'ended') {
    return <SessionEndedView vm={vm} />;
  }

  return <SessionActiveView vm={vm} />;
}

// 2. useSessionPage Hook (State Management)
export function useSessionPage(lessonId: string) {
  const [lesson, setLesson] = useState<Lesson | null>(null);
  const [discussions, setDiscussions] = useState<Discussion[]>([]);
  const [activeDiscussionId, setActiveDiscussionId] = useState<string | null>(null);

  // Setup real-time communication (US 1.34, US 1.27)
  const { channel, isConnected } = useRealtime(lessonId, 'instructor');

  // US 1.06 - Start lesson
  const startLesson = async () => {
    const { data } = await supabase
      .from('lessons')
      .update({
        status: 'active',
        started_at: new Date().toISOString(),
      })
      .eq('id', lessonId)
      .select()
      .single();

    if (data) {
      setLesson(data);
    }
  };

  // US 1.28 - Start discussion
  const startDiscussion = async (discussionId: string) => {
    const updated = await discussionService.publish(discussionId);

    // Broadcast to students (US 1.27 - AT2)
    channel?.send({
      type: 'broadcast',
      event: 'discussion-started',
      payload: {
        discussionId: updated.id,
        prompt: updated.prompt_text,
      },
    });

    setActiveDiscussionId(discussionId);
  };

  // US 1.09 - End lesson
  const endLesson = async () => {
    // Close all active discussions (US 1.09 - AT4)
    await Promise.all(
      discussions
        .filter(d => d.status === 'active')
        .map(d => discussionService.close(d.id))
    );

    // Update lesson status (US 1.09 - AT1)
    const { data } = await supabase
      .from('lessons')
      .update({
        status: 'ended',
        ended_at: new Date().toISOString(),
      })
      .eq('id', lessonId)
      .select()
      .single();

    // Broadcast to students (US 1.09 - AT2, AT3)
    channel?.send({
      type: 'broadcast',
      event: 'lesson-ended',
      payload: { lessonId, endedAt: data.ended_at },
    });

    if (data) {
      setLesson(data);
    }
  };

  return {
    lesson,
    discussions,
    startLesson,
    startDiscussion,
    endLesson,
    channel,
    isConnected,
  };
}

// 3. SessionActiveView Component (Presentation)
export function SessionActiveView({ vm }: { vm: SessionViewModel }) {
  return (
    <div className="session-layout">
      <SessionHeaderActive 
        lesson={vm.lesson}
        onEndLesson={vm.endLesson}
      />

      {/* US 1.31 - Display PIN */}
      <JoinCodeOverlay pinCode={vm.lesson.pin_code} />

      {/* US 1.25 - Multiple discussions */}
      <ActiveSidebar 
        discussions={vm.discussions}
        onStartDiscussion={vm.startDiscussion}
      />

      {/* US 1.27 - Display prompt */}
      <ActiveCenter 
        currentDiscussion={vm.activeDiscussion}
      />

      {/* US 1.34 - Real-time responses */}
      <ActiveRightPanel 
        responses={vm.responses}
        isConnected={vm.isConnected}
      />
    </div>
  );
}

// 1. HomeJoin Component (PIN Entry)
export function HomeJoin() {
  const { 
    code, 
    onChangeCode, 
    join, 
    error, 
    pinOk 
  } = useHomeJoin();

  return (
    <Card>
      <CardHeader>
        <CardTitle>Join a session</CardTitle>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        {error && <Alert variant="destructive">{error}</Alert>}

        {/* US 2.06 - AT1: Input PIN */}
        <Input
          value={code}
          onChange={(e) => onChangeCode(e.target.value)}
          placeholder="123456"
        />

        {/* US 2.06 - AT4: Join button */}
        <Button onClick={join} disabled={!pinOk}>
          Join
        </Button>
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  );
}

// 2. useHomeJoin Hook (PIN Validation)
export function useHomeJoin() {
  const [code, setCode] = useState('');
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const router = useRouter();

  const pinOk = code.length === 6;

  const join = async () => {
    setError(null);

    // US 2.06 - AT3: Validate non-empty
    if (!pinOk) {
      setError('Enter exactly 6 digits');
      return;
    }

    // US 2.06 - AT1: Query for active lesson with PIN
    const { data: lesson, error: queryError } = await supabase
      .from('lessons')
      .select('*')
      .eq('pin_code', code.toUpperCase())
      .eq('status', 'active') // US 2.03 - AT4
      .single();

    if (queryError || !lesson) {
      // US 2.06 - AT2: Invalid PIN
      setError('Invalid PIN or lesson is not active');
      return;
    }

    // US 2.06 - AT4: Navigate to session
    router.push(`/student/${lesson.id}`);
  };

  return {
    code,
    onChangeCode: setCode,
    join,
    error,
    pinOk,
  };
}

// 3. StudentSessionPage Component
export function StudentSessionPage({ lessonId }: { lessonId: string }) {
  const {
    currentDiscussion,
    promptText,
    responseText,
    setResponseText,
    handleSubmit,
    submitting,
    submitted,
  } = useStudentSession(lessonId);

  // US 2.09 - AT1: Display prompt when available
  if (!currentDiscussion) {
    return <StudentWaitingCard />;
  }

  return (
    <StudentSessionShell>
      {/* US 2.09 - AT1, AT3: Show prompt */}
      <StudentPromptCard prompt={promptText} />

      {/* US 2.07: Response form */}
      <StudentResponseForm
        value={responseText}
        onChange={setResponseText}
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        submitting={submitting}
        submitted={submitted}
      />
    </StudentSessionShell>
  );
}

// 4. useStudentSession Hook
export function useStudentSession(lessonId: string) {
  const [currentDiscussion, setCurrentDiscussion] = useState<Discussion | null>(null);
  const [promptText, setPromptText] = useState('');
  const [responseText, setResponseText] = useState('');
  const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false);
  const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState(false);

  // US 2.09 - AT2: Subscribe to real-time updates
  const { channel, isConnected } = useRealtime(lessonId, 'student');

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!channel || !isConnected) return;

    // US 2.09 - AT2: Listen for discussion start
    channel.on('broadcast', { event: 'discussion-started' }, (payload) => {
      setCurrentDiscussion(payload.payload.discussion);
      setPromptText(payload.payload.prompt);
    });

    // US 1.28 - AT3, US 2.07 - AT3: Listen for discussion close
    channel.on('broadcast', { event: 'discussion-closed' }, () => {
      setCurrentDiscussion(null);
    });
  }, [channel, isConnected]);

  // US 2.07 - Submit response
  const handleSubmit = async () => {
    // US 2.07 - AT2: Validate non-blank
    if (!responseText.trim()) {
      alert('Response cannot be blank');
      return;
    }

    // US 2.07 - AT3: Check discussion is active
    if (!currentDiscussion || currentDiscussion.status !== 'active') {
      alert('This discussion is closed');
      return;
    }

    setSubmitting(true);

    try {
      // US 2.07 - AT1: Submit response
      const response = await responseService.submit({
        discussion_id: currentDiscussion.id,
        response_text: responseText.trim(),
      });

      // Broadcast to instructor (US 1.34 - AT1)
      channel?.send({
        type: 'broadcast',
        event: 'response:new',
        payload: { response },
      });

      // US 2.07 - AT4: Show confirmation
      setSubmitted(true);
      setResponseText('');

      setTimeout(() => setSubmitted(false), 2000);

    } catch (error) {
      alert('Failed to submit response');
    } finally {
      setSubmitting(false);
    }
  };

  return {
    currentDiscussion,
    promptText,
    responseText,
    setResponseText,
    handleSubmit,
    submitting,
    submitted,
  };
}

docs.text:
Event: discussion-started

Instructor clicks "Start Discussion"
    │
    ↓
discussionService.publish(discussionId)
    │
    ↓
Update discussion status = 'active'
    │
    ↓
channel.send({
  type: 'broadcast',
  event: 'discussion-started',
  payload: {
    discussionId: string,
    prompt: string,
    promptType: PromptType
  }
})
    │
    ↓
Supabase Realtime Server
    │
    ├──────────────────────────────────────┐
    ↓                                      ↓
Instructor receives                   All Students receive
(UI update - AT2)                     (Show prompt - AT1)
    │                                      │
    ↓                                      ↓
ActiveCenter shows                    StudentPromptCard
current discussion                    renders prompt

Event: response:new

Student submits response
    │
    ↓
responseService.submit(input)
    │
    ↓
Insert into responses table
    │
    ↓
channel.send({
  type: 'broadcast',
  event: 'response:new',
  payload: {
    response: Response
  }
})
    │
    ↓
Supabase Realtime Server
    │
    ↓
Instructor receives
    │
    ↓
ActiveRightPanel.responses.push(newResponse)
    │
    ↓
Response appears without refresh (US 1.34 - AT1)
